name: Qiuchan He
title: AI ENGINEER
profile_summary: AI engineer bridging machine learning and product, with a master's in Data Science & AI. Builds end-to-end features — from Python ML services and REST APIs to polished Angular and React interfaces with a strong eye for UI/UX.

experience.title: AI Engineer
bullets: Build AI-powered product features end-to-end, from Python model services to Angular/React UI.
Design and ship REST APIs connecting ML pipelines to the frontend.
Own UI/UX for AI features, turning model capabilities into intuitive user workflows.

experience.title: Working Student – AI & Development
bullets: Prototyped ML features in Python and integrated them into the product via REST APIs.
Implemented frontend components in Angular and React with a focus on usability.

analysis.explanation: The CV presents a coherent career path, but the rapid progression from a working student to an AI Engineer with end-to-end feature ownership within two years, while still pursuing a Master's degree, raises some questions about the depth of experience claimed. The GitHub profile also shows a mix of projects, some of which are course-related, which aligns with the education but doesn't strongly validate the 'end-to-end' AI Engineer claims.
red_flags: Rapid progression to 'AI Engineer' with end-to-end responsibilities while still completing a Master's degree, potentially overstating practical experience.
GitHub profile contains several course projects, which, while relevant to education, don't fully demonstrate the depth of 'end-to-end' AI feature development claimed in the professional experience.
positive_signals: Skills listed are consistently reflected in the job descriptions.
Timeline for education and experience is consistent with no significant gaps or overlaps.
Certifications align with the stated skills and career path.
LinkedIn URL is reachable, confirming the existence of the profile.
